summary: This abstract summarizes the historical progression of research into the genetics of dizygotic (DZ) twinning. It highlights how decades of collaborative research, including hormone studies, pedigree analyses, and genome-wide association studies (GWAS), ultimately led to the discovery of the first genes predisposing women to DZ twinning.

signature_quotes.text: In the course of twin studies whose main focus was elucidation of genetic and environmental factors on behavioral traits, many twin researchers became aware of the strong tendency for dizygotic (DZ) twinning to run in families.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on the origins of genetic research into DZ twinning.
